full_text:
Immunofluorescence for the organoids

Fixation and storage

Carefully remove the medium without disturbing the droplet. Add 200 µl per well 4% PFA - 0.1 % glutaraldehyde, fix at RT for 30’.

Remove PFA, and wash the wells three times with PBS. Seal the chamber with parafilm after the washes. Remember to leave a large volume of PBS per well.

Staining

Clear organoids using 2.5 M Fructose - 60 % v/v glycerol solution for 20 minutes in RT

Quench the autofluorescence by incubating in a 0.53 M glycine-PBS solution for 30 minutes in RT

Wash once with PBS + 0.45 % Tx100

Add 200 µl per well blocking buffer 🡪 3 % BSA + 2 % TritonX-100 in 1x PBS, incubate at RT for 1 hr.

Remove blocking buffer and add 100 µl / well antibody cocktail prepared in blocking buffer. Incubate overnight at +4 in a humidified chamber.

Next day, remove the antibody mixture, and wash the wells three times with 200 µl of PBS + 0.45 % Tx100. The washes should be at least 5’ long each.

Fluorophores/Antibodies:

DAPI
